description_long: This object is a vintage, manually-operated sewing machine constructed entirely from wood, featuring a simple and rustic design. The main body consists of a rectangular wooden base supporting two vertical posts, which hold a central mechanism with a large, oval-shaped gear and a protruding crank handle. The gear mechanism is exposed, with visible teeth and a lever arm that would be rotated by hand to drive the sewing action. The overall construction is robust, with all parts crafted from the same light brown wood, giving it a uniform appearance. There are no visible electrical components, branding, or modern features, emphasizing its historical and mechanical nature. The surface shows a matte, slightly rough finish, typical of unfinished or lightly sanded wood.
description: A vintage, hand-cranked wooden sewing machine with exposed gear mechanism and a simple, rustic design. All parts are made from light brown wood with a matte finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full gear mechanism, crank handle, and both supporting posts on the wooden base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the wooden components and the profile of the gear and handle.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the gear mechanism and the posts, with less detail on the handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the profile of the gear and the supporting structure, similar to view 1 but from the other side.
